response:
The `/decide` panel ran to completion — inherit, frame, advise (3 fresh advisors, opposing counsel on the two-sided call), PM decision, and an independent verifier gate (PASS, no blockers). The verifier caught one real defect worth flagging separately below.

## Decision

**ADR 0045 accepted: sync-before-spend prose rule; claim protocol deferred to n=2.** Option 1 wins — a one-line rule added to CLAUDE.md's Shipping section, not a claim/comment protocol. The claim protocol is explicitly rejected *for now*, held as the escalation if a second collision (n=2) ever lands. The related foreground-wait subagent lesson is housed inside this ADR rather than a new doc.

```

title: "Sync-before-spend prose rule prevents duplicate parallel issue execution; claim protocol deferred to n=2"
status: accepted
summary: "One-line sync-before-spend rule in CLAUDE.md Shipping (git fetch + re-read issue + search referencing PRs at execution start; repeat before push) prevents duplicate parallel issue execution. Claim protocol deferred to n=2 (per-dispatch tax unjustified at n=1). Foreground-wait subagent lesson houses in this ADR."
---

# 0045 — Sync-before-spend prose rule; claim protocol deferred to n=2

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: lean-process-engineer (waste/poka-yoke), session-operator (live-operator realism), process-economist (cost-per-decision) — the three axes on a cost-vs-prevention rule under a hard doc budget; no adoption/sequencing axis here.
- Context: A dispatched session executed issue #30 in full (~$5-8 nested `claude -p` + agent spend) while `main` already carried the merged fix — PR #97 closed #30 concurrently; caught only at end-of-session `git fetch` (ISSUE-106.md:4-6). Clone fresh-at-start, stale-by-use. Gap: nothing forces a session to re-sync `origin/main` + re-read the target issue's state/referencing PRs before spending, nor leaves a visible claim (ISSUE-106.md:10). Open question: sync-rule / claim-protocol / both, and where the foreground-wait subagent lesson lives.

## Decision
**Option 1 — sync-before-spend, prose only.** One bullet in CLAUDE.md Shipping (after the squash-merge/fetch bullet, CLAUDE.md:53-57): before executing an issue `git fetch` + re-read it + search PRs referencing it (a concurrent merge may already have closed it); repeat before final push. The **at-start** check gates spend — fires BEFORE the $5-8, not after — so it is prevention at the point that matters, and the referencing-PR search is the exact mechanism that surfaces an in-flight PR #97 for this incident class. **Reject the claim protocol at n=1**; hold as the n=2 escalation (Revisit). **Foreground-wait lesson houses in THIS ADR** (below).

Foreground-wait lesson (its home): subagent dispatch prompts should mandate foreground waits on long child runs — a detached-background subagent stalled twice until nudged (ISSUE-106.md:18). No dispatch-prompt doc owns this today; a doc for one lesson is gold-plating, and CLAUDE.md is budget-tight + wrong altitude (subagent-dispatch detail, not a shipping/docs fact). Relocate to a real dispatch doc when one exists (Revisit).

## Justification
Cost x risk x value. **Effort:** option 1 is one sentence extending an existing precedent (CLAUDE.md:53-54 already mandates fetch-before-rebase for ranging/branching) — incremental, not novel machinery. The claim protocol is a new protocol PLUS a permission fallback: dispatch sometimes withholds issue-edit access ("don't change the issues", ISSUE-106.md:15), and comment-only permission isn't verified in any actual dispatch template (none in-repo) — so "both" always adds cost on top of 1, the fallback still needing the prose rule. **Value:** n=1, no prior collision in git log, no dispatch/queue doc makes concurrent same-issue dispatch routine — edge case, not structural pattern; the claim tax is paid EVERY dispatch against a benefit that fired once. **Risk:** CLAUDE.md had 278 chars headroom (ADR 0008 budget); the sync bullet fits, a claim rule with its permission carve-out likely won't without demotion. session-operator's "sync detects, doesn't prevent" (contra CLAUDE.md:22) is answered by the reframe: the at-start checkpoint gates spend, not reports post-hoc; a scripted gate (true poka-yoke) is the n=1-unjustified escalation (CLAUDE.md:21 premature machinery).

## Assumptions
- [verified] CLAUDE.md is 5,917 chars post-edit (< 6,000 cap; code-point count matching the gate's own measure in char-budget.mjs:31-32 / ADR 0008) — recomputed after this ADR's edit; the sync bullet fits.
- [verified] The fetch-before-rebase precedent this extends is scoped to "ranging, branching, or /retrospect", NOT issue-execution spend (CLAUDE.md:53-54) — the gap ISSUE-106.md:10 names is real; no existing rule closes it.
- [checkable] No in-repo dispatch/queue doc assigns concurrent sessions to one backlog; git log shows no prior same-issue collision — owner: verifier; result: confirmed at decision (grep found none); grounds "edge case, not pattern".
- [unverifiable] Same-issue parallel collisions are rare enough that a compliance-uncertain prose rule beats a guaranteed per-dispatch coordination tax — no base rate at n=1. REOPEN-IF: a second collision lands (Revisit).

## Rejected alternatives
- **Claim protocol (2) now** — per-dispatch tax + permission fallback + its own stale-claim poka-yoke (session dies mid-run), for an event seen once; all it adds over option 1 is the long-running-collision case. Held as the n=2 escalation.
- **Both (3) now** — the fallback still needs the prose rule, so "both" is option 1 plus option-2 cost, never a substitute; premature + budget-infeasible.
- **A scripted pre-spend gate** — true poka-yoke, but building + testing a gate ("no gate without a test", CLAUDE.md:43) at n=1 is gold-plating.
- **A new doc for the foreground-wait lesson** — a doc for one lesson is gold-plating; the ADR is its home until a real dispatch doc exists.

## Revisit triggers
- **A second same-issue collision (n=2)** → escalate to the claim protocol and/or a scripted pre-spend gate; the "rare enough" bet is refuted.
- **A dispatch/queue doc that routinely assigns concurrent sessions to one backlog** → collisions turn structural; re-price the claim protocol now, don't wait for n=2.
- **A real dispatch-prompt doc is created** → relocate the foreground-wait lesson here into it (one-home rule).
```

CLAUDE.md's Shipping section now carries the new bullet (line 58): *"**Sync before spend** (ADR 0045): before executing an issue `git fetch` + re-read it + search PRs referencing it (a concurrent merge may already have closed it); repeat before final push — a clone fresh-at-start goes stale by use."* File is at 5,917 chars against the 6,000 cap.

**One thing surfaced along the way, unrelated to this decision:** the post-edit adr-lint gate hook flagged a real, pre-existing problem — `0026-benchmark-artifact-efficiency.md` amends ADR 0023 but 0023 doesn't cite 0026 back (unpointed amendment). Confirmed via `git status` that both files are untouched by this work — it's drift from an earlier PR, not introduced here. Per the one-concern-per-PR rule I didn't fix it in this pass; it needs its own fix.

Nothing is committed — CLAUDE.md is modified and `docs/decisions/0045-sync-before-spend-issue-execution.md` is a new untracked file, both sitting in the working tree for your review.

--- recorded decision file docs/decisions/0045-sync-before-spend-issue-execution.md ---
